name: Synaptic.io
one_liner: AI inference routing platform that automatically selects the cheapest model for each query without degrading quality.
problem: Companies running LLM applications are shocked when their inference bills arrive. GPT-4o costs 100x more than GPT-4o-mini for tasks that don't require frontier-level reasoning.
solution: Synaptic.io proxies all LLM API calls, classifies query complexity, routes simple queries to cheaper models, and guarantees response quality via an automated eval layer. We project 60-80% inference cost reduction.
founder_background: MBA from Wharton, 6 years in product management at Oracle. No technical co-founder identified yet. Has not shipped software before.

name: StackPilot
one_liner: AI-powered code review that catches security vulnerabilities and architecture drift before they reach production.
problem: Engineering teams at 50-500 person companies lack the bandwidth for thorough code reviews. Security vulnerabilities slip through, and architectural debt compounds silently.
solution: StackPilot integrates into GitHub and GitLab pull request workflows and runs automated reviews using fine-tuned LLMs trained on each team's own codebase conventions and security policies.
founder_background: Ex-Google engineer (L6), 8 years on Google Cloud security tooling. Previously built and sold a developer productivity SaaS to Atlassian. Two co-founders, both technical.

name: LatticeDB
one_liner: The only vector database built from scratch for multi-tenant AI applications at enterprise scale.
problem: Existing vector databases were not designed for multi-tenancy. Enterprises building RAG applications for multiple clients face data isolation problems, unpredictable latency, and 10x over-provisioning costs.
solution: LatticeDB's engine provides per-tenant isolation with a shared compute pool, automated index partitioning, and query cost governance — cutting enterprise AI infrastructure costs by 60-70%.
founder_background: Two co-founders: ex-SingleStore VP of Engineering and an ex-Databricks staff engineer who authored two published papers on vector index structures at Stanford.

name: Redline AI
one_liner: Behavioral anomaly detection for cloud workloads that eliminates 80% of SOC alert noise without human tuning.
problem: Security operations centers are drowning in false positive alerts. The average enterprise gets 11,000 alerts per day; 45% are false positives. Analyst burnout and missed true positives are the result.
solution: Redline's graph-based behavioral model learns normal workload patterns per cluster, correlates signals across network, IAM, and compute layers, and surfaces only confirmed anomaly chains — reducing alert volume by 80% in controlled pilots.
founder_background: CEO was Head of Detection Engineering at CrowdStrike for 5 years. CTO holds two patents in behavioral ML for network anomaly detection and previously ran research at Darktrace.

name: Meridian Workflows
one_liner: LLM orchestration platform that lets enterprises build, monitor, and govern multi-agent AI workflows without writing orchestration code.
problem: Enterprises want to deploy multi-agent AI systems for back-office automation but lack safe, auditable orchestration infrastructure. Custom pipelines break, hallucinations go undetected, and compliance teams can't audit agent decisions.
solution: Meridian provides a visual workflow builder with LLM-agnostic routing, real-time evaluation, rollback controls, and a compliance audit trail that satisfies SOC 2 and emerging EU AI Act requirements.
founder_background: CEO was VP of Product at UiPath during its hypergrowth phase (Series C to IPO). CTO co-founded an AI safety startup acquired by Anthropic in 2024. Team of 18, $1.8M ARR.

name: ContextHub
one_liner: Context management infrastructure for LLM applications — persist, retrieve, and version application memory without rebuilding your stack.
problem: Developers building stateful LLM applications spend 30-40% of engineering time on context window management: what to include, how to compress, what to persist. There is no standard abstraction layer.
solution: ContextHub is an open-source SDK with a managed cloud tier. It handles context compression, memory tiering (hot/warm/cold), retrieval routing, and cross-session persistence across any LLM provider.
founder_background: Solo founder with 6 years at AWS Lambda on the execution environment team. Open-source repo has 2.8K GitHub stars after 6 weeks. Looking for a second co-founder.

name: VaultShield
one_liner: Zero-trust network access for SMBs that replaces legacy VPN infrastructure in under 30 minutes.
problem: SMBs with 20-200 employees can't afford Zscaler or Prisma Access but face the same remote-work and supply-chain attack surface as enterprises. Legacy VPNs are frequently exploited.
solution: VaultShield is a cloud-native ZTNA solution priced at $8/user/month. Deployment requires no hardware changes and fits in an afternoon, managed via a single-pane dashboard built for non-security IT admins.
founder_background: CEO was Head of Security Product at JumpCloud. COO ran SMB sales at 1Password and grew that segment from $2M to $40M ARR. 140 paying customers, $68K MRR.

name: PhantomGuard
one_liner: Continuous software supply chain security scanning that tracks every open-source dependency from merge to production.
problem: SBOM generation is now mandated in US federal contracts and increasingly required by enterprise buyers, but most dev teams have no tooling to generate, maintain, or alert on SBOMs continuously.
solution: PhantomGuard integrates into CI/CD pipelines to generate living SBOMs, tracks transitive dependency mutations, maps them to CVEs in real time, and routes remediation actions directly to the responsible engineering team.
founder_background: Two co-founders from MITRE Corporation's cybersecurity division. One has DARPA-funded research background in software provenance; the other built supply chain security tooling at GitHub for 4 years.

name: Cipherline
one_liner: Continuous compliance automation for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA that replaces $300K/year consultant engagements.
problem: Mid-market SaaS companies spend 6-12 months and $200-400K on their first SOC 2 audit. Evidence collection is mostly manual and compliance lapses between annual audits create real liability.
solution: Cipherline connects to 150+ cloud infrastructure and SaaS tools, pulls evidence automatically, maps it to control frameworks, and generates a continuously updated audit-ready posture report with direct integration into Big 4 auditor portals.
founder_background: CEO was a Partner at Deloitte's cybersecurity practice for 9 years. CTO built the compliance automation engine at Drata before founding Cipherline. $1.4M ARR, 110 customers.

name: ClearLedger
one_liner: Automated bookkeeping for SMBs that closes the books 10 days faster by connecting to bank feeds and reconciling in real time.
problem: 75% of small businesses still use an external bookkeeper for month-end reconciliation, costing $500-2,000/month and producing financial insight too late to act on.
solution: ClearLedger connects to 3,000+ US bank accounts via Plaid, auto-categorizes transactions using a fine-tuned model trained on 12M SMB transactions, and generates real-time P&L and cash flow projections visible to both the owner and their accountant.
founder_background: CEO is a CPA who spent 10 years at EY before building and selling an accounting SaaS to Intuit. CTO previously built Plaid's transaction categorization engine. $95K MRR, 820 customers.

name: NovaPay
one_liner: Cross-border payment rails for Africa and Southeast Asia that settle in under 4 seconds at a 0.5% FX spread.
problem: SMEs and freelancers in Nigeria, Kenya, Indonesia, and the Philippines face 5-8% FX spreads and 3-5 day settlement times. Traditional correspondent banking rails are expensive and opaque.
solution: NovaPay uses local stablecoin liquidity pools in each corridor, combined with local banking partnerships, to offer real-time settlement at sub-1% cost. Founders operated the liquidity infrastructure for 18 months before productizing it.
founder_background: CEO is a second-time founder whose previous company was acquired by Paystack. COO was Remitly's Country Manager for West Africa. $3.2M GMV/month across 3 corridors.

name: Thrift AI
one_liner: AI-powered personal finance app for Gen Z that turns paycheck-to-paycheck earners into consistent savers.
problem: 62% of Americans under 35 live paycheck to paycheck. Traditional finance apps show you what you spent but none proactively negotiate bills, find savings opportunities, or build behavioral habits.
solution: Thrift connects to bank and credit card accounts, identifies recurring charges, negotiates lower bills on the user's behalf, and delivers micro-savings nudges tied to specific spending patterns.
founder_background: First-time founders. CEO has a UX design background from IDEO; COO worked on Credit Karma's growth team. 4,200 waitlist sign-ups. No product shipped yet.

name: OncoPal
one_liner: Clinical trial matching platform that connects oncology patients to eligible trials 6x faster than the current standard of care.
problem: Only 5% of adult cancer patients participate in clinical trials and 80% of trials fail to meet enrollment timelines. The manual matching process takes 2-4 weeks per patient and is plagued by missed eligibility criteria.
solution: OncoPal's NLP engine parses unstructured EHR data, maps it to eligibility criteria across 3,000+ open oncology trials on ClinicalTrials.gov, and surfaces ranked matches to the care team in real time via Epic and Cerner plugins.
founder_background: CEO is an oncologist from Dana-Farber who ran a clinical trial matching program manually for 7 years. CTO has a PhD in biomedical NLP from MIT. 3 design partner hospitals signed.

name: Vitatrack
one_liner: Remote patient monitoring SaaS that integrates with 40+ wearable devices and reduces hospital readmissions by 34%.
problem: Patients with CHF, COPD, and diabetes have a 20-25% 30-day readmission rate. Health systems pay $5,000-15,000 per readmission penalty under Medicare. Existing RPM solutions require proprietary hardware and lack EHR integration.
solution: Vitatrack aggregates consumer wearable data (Apple Watch, Garmin, Fitbit, Dexcom), applies ML-based deterioration models, and sends structured alerts to care coordinators via a mobile dashboard with bidirectional Epic integration.
founder_background: CEO spent 12 years at Medtronic building remote monitoring systems. CMO is a cardiologist and former HHS digital health policy advisor. $1.1M ARR, 8 health system customers.

name: MindBridge
one_liner: Employer-sponsored mental health platform that goes beyond EAP coverage to offer personalized, stigma-free care pathways for employees.
problem: EAP programs have a 3-6% utilization rate. Mental health conditions cost US employers $200B/year in lost productivity. Existing solutions (BetterHelp, Lyra, Spring Health) are expensive and require heavy HR admin.
solution: MindBridge offers a $35/PEPM self-serve employer platform with AI-driven intake triage, a network of 2,000 therapists and coaches, async text coaching for mild conditions, and anonymized utilization dashboards for HR.
founder_background: CEO is a licensed therapist who ran a corporate wellness program at Goldman Sachs for 4 years. CTO has a background in ML for healthcare from Stanford. No product shipped; two Fortune 500 design partners in discussion.

name: CarbonPulse
one_liner: Scope 3 emissions data platform that gives enterprises audit-ready supply chain carbon accounting in 72 hours instead of 9 months.
problem: SEC climate disclosure rules and the EU CSRD require large enterprises to report Scope 3 emissions starting in 2025. Existing methodology is survey-based, takes 9 months, and produces estimates that don't hold up to external audit.
solution: CarbonPulse connects to enterprise ERP, procurement, and logistics systems and maps spend and shipment data to a proprietary emissions factor database (12M SKUs and logistics lanes), generating an audit-grade Scope 3 report compatible with GHG Protocol.
founder_background: CEO was McKinsey climate practice lead for 8 years and led Scope 3 implementations at 3 Fortune 50 companies. CTO built emissions data infrastructure at Project Canary. 5 pilots running.

name: Solarray
one_liner: Solar financing marketplace that connects residential homeowners with pre-underwritten loan and PACE products in under 15 minutes.
problem: 72% of US homeowners express interest in solar but never convert because the financing process takes 6-8 weeks and involves 3-5 separate parties. Installers lose 40% of qualified leads during financing.
solution: Solarray is the solar financing OS for installers: a white-labeled marketplace that surfaces pre-underwritten products from 18 lenders, runs real-time underwriting, and sends DocuSign packages to the homeowner — all from the installer's existing CRM.
founder_background: CEO previously ran the installer channel at Sunrun ($1.5B in originations handled). CTO built lending infrastructure at LendingClub. $2.8M ARR, 140 installer customers, 22% MoM growth.

name: Greenthread
one_liner: Textile supply chain traceability platform that gives fashion brands blockchain-verified fiber-to-retail sustainability data.
problem: Greenwashing regulations in the EU and US now require fashion brands to substantiate sustainability claims with evidence. Most supply chain visibility tools stop at Tier 1 suppliers; claims are unverifiable below Tier 2.
solution: Greenthread uses QR-tagged yarn barcodes, IoT sensors at dyeing and weaving facilities, and a permissioned blockchain to create an unbroken chain of custody from raw fiber to finished garment.
founder_background: Founder worked in sustainability consulting for LVMH and H&M for 6 years. Technical co-founder is from MIT Media Lab IoT group. No product built. Exploring concept with one potential design partner.

name: LearnLoop
one_liner: Adaptive learning platform for K-12 math and science that uses spaced repetition to double long-term retention.
problem: US students spend $12B/year on tutoring yet 2/3 of 8th graders are below proficiency in math. Most tutoring apps are homework helpers, not mastery builders — they don't target the forgetting curve.
solution: LearnLoop identifies each student's knowledge gaps from their school's LMS data, builds a personalized review queue using SM-2 spaced repetition, and delivers 5-minute daily practice sets reaching parents who can't afford $60/hour tutors.
founder_background: CEO has a PhD in cognitive science (learning and memory) from University of Michigan. CTO was an early engineer at Khan Academy. 6,800 waitlist families, mostly from parent Facebook groups.

name: CertBuilder
one_liner: B2B SaaS platform that lets professional associations and software vendors launch their own certification programs without building learning infrastructure.
problem: Professional associations, software vendors, and trade organizations want to offer certification programs but face $400K+ and 18-month timelines to build custom LMS and credentialing systems.
solution: CertBuilder is a white-labeled platform for course authoring, proctored testing, blockchain-verified digital credentials, and analytics — launched in 3 weeks, not 18 months.
founder_background: CEO built the certification product at Coursera for 5 years. COO ran sales at Credly (digital credentials, acquired by Pearson for $200M). 85 paying customers, $72K MRR.

name: Luma Social
one_liner: Creator monetization platform for micro-influencers (10K-500K followers) that turns brand partnerships into recurring revenue streams.
problem: 94% of influencers with under 500K followers earn less than $10K/year from brand deals. Agencies ignore them; brand marketplaces offer one-off deals. Micro-influencers have engaged audiences but no tools for sustainable monetization.
solution: Luma provides a self-serve platform for micro-influencers to create memberships, sell content bundles, and manage brand partnerships — including a matching engine that connects micro-influencers to relevant brands based on audience overlap rather than follower count.
founder_background: CEO was Head of Creator Partnerships at TikTok US. COO built the monetization product at Patreon. 3,400 active creators, $180K MRR, growing 25% MoM.

name: Nouri
one_liner: Hyperlocal grocery marketplace connecting urban households to neighborhood specialty food vendors for same-hour delivery.
problem: Urban neighborhoods are full of specialty food vendors (Korean markets, Caribbean butchers, Middle Eastern bakeries) that have loyal local customers but no digital presence and lose foot traffic to chain stores with Instacart.
solution: Nouri onboards neighborhood specialty vendors with a 30-minute tablet setup, provides real-time inventory management, handles demand aggregation, and routes deliveries to independent gig workers already in the area.
founder_background: Founder ran an immigrant business support nonprofit in Flatbush, Brooklyn for 4 years. No prior technical background; CTO is a former DoorDash engineer. 22 vendor partners onboarded.

name: Ripple
one_liner: The marketplace where real people share real experiences.
problem: People want authentic experiences but struggle to find them online.
solution: Ripple lets anyone list any experience they can offer and anyone else book it. We are building the Airbnb of experiences.
founder_background: Lifelong entrepreneur with a passion for connecting people. Has started two previous companies that didn't work out. No relevant marketplace or technology background.

name: DockMaster
one_liner: Port logistics management SaaS that reduces dwell time by 28% for mid-size container terminals through real-time berth and yard optimization.
problem: Mid-size container terminals operate with scheduling systems built in the 1990s. Berth allocation, yard positioning, and gate scheduling are handled in spreadsheets, creating 6-12 hour inefficiencies that cost $2,000-8,000 per ship call.
solution: DockMaster's digital twin engine ingests AIS vessel tracking data, ERP cargo manifests, and yard sensor feeds to run continuous optimization across berth, crane, and yard operations — with a tablet app for operators and web dashboard for management.
founder_background: CEO spent 14 years at Maersk, last role as VP of Terminal Operations Americas. CTO has a PhD in operations research from Georgia Tech. $900K ARR, 7 terminal customers.

name: Auditly
one_liner: Continuous control monitoring platform for financial services firms that reduces internal audit labor by 60% and cuts regulatory examination findings.
problem: US banks, broker-dealers, and insurance companies spend $70B/year on compliance. Internal audit teams test controls on quarterly cycles. Between audits, control failures go undetected and compound.
solution: Auditly connects to core banking, trading, and HR systems, runs automated control tests daily (not quarterly), surfaces emerging violations before regulators see them, and generates examination-ready workpapers in PCAOB and OCC-preferred formats.
founder_background: CEO was Chief Compliance Officer at Deutsche Bank Americas. CTO built the surveillance and control testing platform at FINRA. $8.5M ARR, 34 financial institution customers, 3 Big 4 partnerships. Team of 68.

name: Flowdesk
one_liner: Async communication platform that replaces Slack overload for remote teams with structured topic threads, AI-generated daily digests, and a persistent decision log.
problem: Slack creates information overload and a culture of real-time interruptions that destroys deep work. 63% of remote workers report being unable to focus due to messaging volume. Institutional decisions get lost in ephemeral chat history.
solution: Flowdesk organizes communication into persistent topic channels with a forced async format, AI summaries that surface decisions and action items each morning, and a decision log that's searchable and linkable from any conversation.
founder_background: CEO built and sold a remote team management tool to HubSpot in 2021. CTO was an early engineer at Notion. 480 paying teams, $85K MRR, 118% NRR.

name: Harbinger Analytics
one_liner: Predictive maintenance platform for discrete manufacturing that reduces unplanned downtime by 40% using edge-deployed ML on existing machine sensors.
problem: Unplanned equipment downtime costs discrete manufacturers $50B/year in the US. Existing predictive maintenance solutions require proprietary sensors and $2M+ implementation projects, excluding 95% of the market.
solution: Harbinger deploys ML models at the edge (on a $200 NVIDIA Jetson module) that connect to existing PLC and SCADA sensor feeds, learn normal operating signatures, and predict failures 2-5 days in advance — with no cloud connectivity required for real-time inference.
founder_background: CEO is a former Bosch manufacturing executive. CTO holds 4 patents in embedded ML for industrial systems and spent 8 years at Intel's IoT group. $1.7M ARR, 14 manufacturing facility customers.

name: RouteIQ
one_liner: Last-mile delivery route optimization for regional carriers that cuts cost-per-delivery by 22% without replacing existing dispatch systems.
problem: Regional carriers with 100-2,000 vehicles are squeezed between Amazon's private fleet and large 3PLs. They can't afford Oracle TMS or Manhattan Associates and lose 15-25% of gross margin to inefficient routing and manual dispatch.
solution: RouteIQ plugs into existing dispatch tools via API and a tablet app, runs a constraint-based optimizer that re-sequences routes dynamically as conditions change, and provides a driver app for proof-of-delivery capture.
founder_background: CEO ran operations for a 300-truck regional carrier for 7 years. CTO has a PhD in combinatorial optimization from Carnegie Mellon and spent 5 years at FedEx's logistics science team. 4 active pilots.

name: Palante Data
one_liner: Data mesh orchestration platform that lets large enterprises federate analytical ownership across business domains without creating a governance crisis.
problem: Enterprises with 5+ data teams are abandoning centralized data warehouses for federated data mesh architecture, but data mesh creates a governance vacuum: inconsistent definitions, broken lineage, and cross-domain query failures.
solution: Palante provides the governance and catalog layer above a data mesh: universal data contracts, automated lineage tracking across domain boundaries, a federated query router, and a data product marketplace where teams publish and subscribe to datasets with SLA commitments.
founder_background: CEO was VP of Data Platform at Airbnb and a founding engineer at Databricks. CTO led the data governance product at Collibra. $6.2M ARR, 28 Fortune 500 customers. Team of 82.

name: Ferrex Robotics
one_liner: Autonomous piece-picking robot for 3PL and e-commerce fulfillment that handles 95% of SKU variety without reprogramming.
problem: E-commerce order volumes require piece-picking at scale. Manual picking costs $0.80-1.20/pick. Competing robots require weeks of SKU training and fail on novel packaging. 3PLs face labor shortages and Amazon margin pressure simultaneously.
solution: Ferrex's grasping AI uses a self-supervised learning loop to handle new product types within 2 hours of first encounter. The robot integrates with any WMS via standard API, deploys in 10 working days, and is sold as a Robots-as-a-Service subscription at $0.45/pick.
founder_background: CEO has a PhD in robotics from Carnegie Mellon and spent 8 years at Boston Dynamics. CTO co-invented the grasping perception system and holds 11 patents. $12M ARR, 6 3PL customers, $8M ARR in signed LOIs. Team of 95.

name: NanoFab
one_liner: MEMS-based biosensor platform for continuous at-home monitoring of cardiac biomarkers, enabling earlier detection of acute cardiac events.
problem: Heart attack mortality is 50% higher for patients who present to ERs more than 12 hours after onset. Standard troponin blood tests require lab equipment and take 1-2 hours. There is no at-home continuous cardiac biomarker monitor.
solution: NanoFab's MEMS chip uses electrochemical impedance spectroscopy to detect troponin I, BNP, and CRP from interstitial fluid via a wearable patch. The chip is produced using standard CMOS fabrication at a foundry cost of $2.10/chip at scale.
founder_background: Both founders hold PhDs from UCSD in nanoelectronics. Published 4 peer-reviewed papers on MEMS biosensing. Two design partner agreements with cardiologists signed. FDA pre-submission meeting completed.

name: Voxium
one_liner: AI for the future of intelligence infrastructure.
problem: Companies today are not using AI to its full potential. The future requires intelligent systems that can think for themselves.
solution: We are building the next generation of AI infrastructure that will power the thinking machines of tomorrow. Our platform enables any company to become an AI-first organization using our proprietary intelligence layer.
founder_background: Serial entrepreneur passionate about AI. Has worked at multiple startups in the Bay Area. Believes AI is the future. No shipped product, no co-founder, no technical background.

name: Synapse Collective
one_liner: Web3 community platform with staking rewards for content creators.
problem: Content creators are not fairly compensated by Web2 platforms. Centralized platforms extract too much value from creators.
solution: We are building a decentralized content platform where creators mint content as NFTs and earn passive income through our proprietary DeFi staking mechanism. Originally a music NFT marketplace, we pivoted to DeFi staking after the NFT market crashed.
founder_background: First-time founder and crypto enthusiast since 2017. Previously worked in digital marketing. No technical background. Pivoted business model twice in 14 months.

name: BuildRight
one_liner: Construction project management software for general contractors.
problem: Construction project management is still done on paper and spreadsheets in many companies.
solution: BuildRight is a cloud-based project management tool for construction teams. It lets you track tasks, manage budgets, and communicate with subcontractors in one place.
founder_background: First-time founder with a background in construction management. Learned to code on YouTube six months ago. No co-founder. No product shipped. Competing directly with Procore and Buildertrend with no differentiation.

name: MindfulBrew
one_liner: AI-personalized wellness tea subscription box delivered monthly.
problem: People are stressed and overwhelmed and want wellness products but don't know what to buy.
solution: We mail customers a curated selection of wellness teas each month, chosen by our AI system that learns their taste preferences and stress patterns from a short quiz. We are building the Spotify of tea.
founder_background: Tea enthusiast and wellness blogger. 12,000 Instagram followers. No technical background. No co-founder. Has not built a company before.
